nodes.x: So these three men ceased to answer Job, because he was righteous in his own eyes.
Moreover the Lord answered Job, and said,
And they could not answer him again to these things.
Moreover Job continued his parable, and said,
And they could not take hold of his words before the people: and they marvelled at his answer, and held their peace.
Though these three men, Noah, Daniel, and Job, were in it, they should deliver but their own souls by their righteousness, saith the Lord God.
Then was kindled the wrath of Elihu the son of Barachel the Buzite, of the kindred of Ram: against Job was his wrath kindled, because he justified himself rather than God.
Therefore was the wrath of the Lord kindled against his people, insomuch that he abhorred his own inheritance.
When Elihu saw that there was no answer in the mouth of these three men, then his wrath was kindled.
Also against his three friends was his wrath kindled, because they had found no answer, and yet had condemned Job.
¶ And God’s anger was kindled because he went: and the angel of the Lord stood in the way for an adversary against him. Now he was riding upon his ass, and his two servants were with him.
And it came to pass, when his master heard the words of his wife, which she spake unto him, saying, After this manner did thy servant to me; that his wrath was kindled.
Also against his three friends was his wrath kindled, because they had found no answer, and yet had condemned Job.
And the anger of the Lord was kindled against them; and he departed.
Then was kindled the wrath of Elihu the son of Barachel the Buzite, of the kindred of Ram: against Job was his wrath kindled, because he justified himself rather than God.
Now Elihu had waited till Job had spoken, because they were elder than he.
Elihu also proceeded, and said,

But Elisha sat in his house, and the elders sat with him; and the king sent a man from before him: but ere the messenger came to him, he said to the elders, See ye how this son of a murderer hath sent to take away mine head? look, when the messenger cometh, shut the door, and hold him fast at the door: is not the sound of his master’s feet behind him?
Furthermore Elihu answered and said,
But he went in, and stood before his master. And Elisha said unto him, Whence comest thou, Gehazi? And he said, Thy servant went no whither.
And the king talked with Gehazi the servant of the man of God, saying, Tell me, I pray thee, all the great things that Elisha hath done.
When Elihu saw that there was no answer in the mouth of these three men, then his wrath was kindled.
And in nothing doth man offend God, or against none is his wrath kindled, save those who confess not his hand in all things, and obey not his commandments.
And Elihu the son of Barachel the Buzite answered and said, I am young, and ye are very old; wherefore I was afraid, and durst not shew you mine opinion.
Now Elihu had waited till Job had spoken, because they were elder than he.
31 And his sons answered him., saying, Before the Lord we will walk according to His law.

that have been millions of ages in progress, also the faculty of seeing and comprehending that which will take place in the future ages of eternity, for millions of years to come. Here we prophesy in part, and here we have knowledge in part; here we gaze upon one thing at once; here we can comprehend the future in some measure. But we “see through a glass darkly,” then we shall see face to face;…
Then answered Eliphaz the Temanite, and said,
And he said, Behold now, I am old, I know not the day of my death:
I said, Days should speak, and multitude of years should teach wisdom.
So teach us to number our days, that we may apply our hearts unto wisdom.
With the ancient is wisdom; and in length of days understanding.
¶ All this have I proved by wisdom: I said, I will be wise; but it was far from me.
But we speak the wisdom of God in a mystery, even the hidden wisdom, which God ordained before the world unto our glory:
I speak as to wise men; judge ye what I say.
But there is a spirit in man: and the inspiration of the Almighty giveth them understanding.

There is in man a spirit — the inspiration of the Almighty. He has planted in the soul a thirst for excellence — an irresistible impulse to progress. He moves upon the mind to interrogate nature for her

In every part and portion of the revelations of God as given to the
children of men, or to any individual in heaven or on earth, to
understand them properly, a man needs the Spirit by which they were
given--the Spirit that reveals such matters to the understanding, and
makes them familiar to the mind. 8:27.
He that hath knowledge spareth his words: and a man of understanding is of an excellent spirit.
Neither is man capable to make them known, for they are only to be seen and understood by the power of the Holy Spirit, which God bestows on those who love him, and purify themselves before him;
Therefore, whoso readeth it, let him understand, for the Spirit manifesteth truth;
Great men are not always wise: neither do the aged understand judgment.
Wise men lay up knowledge: but the mouth of the foolish is near destruction.
I speak to your shame. Is it so, that there is not a wise man among you? no, not one that shall be able to judge between his brethren?
Therefore I said, Hearken to me; I also will shew mine opinion.
I said, I will answer also my part, I also will shew mine opinion.
I will shew thee, hear me; and that which I have seen I will declare;
Nevertheless hear thou now this word that I speak in thine ears, and in the ears of all the people;
But I say unto you, That every idle word that men shall speak, they shall give account thereof in the day of judgment.
Behold, I waited for your words; I gave ear to your reasons, whilst ye searched out what to say.
Behold, I waited for your words; I gave ear to your reasons, whilst ye searched out what to say.
Hear now my reasoning, and hearken to the pleadings of my lips.
Give ear to my words, O Lord, consider my meditation.
Therefore I said, Hearken to me; I also will shew mine opinion.
¶ Give ye ear, and hear my voice; hearken, and hear my speech.
Unto me men gave ear, and waited, and kept silence at my counsel.
Yea, I attended unto you, and, behold, there was none of you that convinced Job, or that answered his words:
And he answered: Nay; Lord, show thyself unto me.
They say unto him, Because no man hath hired us. He saith unto them, Go ye also into the vineyard; and whatsoever is right, that shall ye receive.
But he answered and said, Verily I say unto you, I know you not.
And he answered and said: Yea, I believe all the words that thou hast taught.
Then Job answered the Lord, and said,
Lest ye should say, We have found out wisdom: God thrusteth him down, not man.
Wisdom is before him that hath understanding; but the eyes of a fool are in the ends of the earth.
Now, behold this is wisdom; whoso readeth, let him understand and receive also;
If any of you lack wisdom, let him ask of God, that giveth to all men liberally, and upbraideth not; and it shall be given him.
For the Lord giveth wisdom: out of his mouth cometh knowledge and understanding.
Now he hath not directed his words against me: neither will I answer him with your speeches.
Sound speech, that cannot be condemned; that he that is of the contrary part may be ashamed, having no evil thing to say of you.
And he answered and said, Must I not take heed to speak that which the Lord hath put in my mouth?

Then I said, I will not make mention of him, nor speak any more in his name. But his word was in mine heart as a burning fire shut up in my bones, and I was weary with forbearing, and I could not stay.

I said to him, “ Ask himself, sir, whether from 
the time that he has entered my house I have 
done anything improper, or have offended him 
in any respect.” He answered, “I also know 
that you neither have done nor will do anything 
improper, and therefore I speak these words to 
you, that you may persevere. For he had a good 
report of you to me, and you will say these 
words to others, that…
They were amazed, they answered no more: they left off speaking.
And they answered, that they could not tell whence it was.

"Yes," they replied, with one unanimous voice. And they strictly kept
their faith to the end of the journey.

"’No, I did not, dear,’ I answered.  ’But come and sit down.  We will
say no more about it.’
Then I answered them, The word of the Lord came unto me, saying,
Then he questioned with him in many words; but he answered him nothing.
When I had waited, (for they spake not, but stood still, and answered no more;)
They were amazed, they answered no more: they left off speaking.
I waited patiently for the Lord; and he inclined unto me, and heard my cry.
Yet they sent unto me four times after this sort; and I answered them after the same manner.
I said, I will answer also my part, I also will shew mine opinion.
How much less shall I answer him, and choose out my words to reason with him?
And ye answered me, and said, The thing which thou hast spoken is good for us to do.
For I am full of matter, the spirit within me constraineth me.
Nevertheless, in your temporal things you shall be equal, and this not grudgingly, otherwise the abundance of the manifestations of the Spirit shall be withheld.

looking at it philosophically we shall find a very maieriat difference between the body and the spirit: — the body is supposed to bo organized matter, and tho spirit by many is thought to be immaterial, without substance. With this latter statement we should beg leave to differ— and state that spirit is a substance; that it is material, but that it is more pure, elastic, and refined matter than…
And a portion of that Spirit dwelleth in me, which giveth me knowledge, and also power according to my faith and desires which are in God.
Behold, my belly is as wine which hath no vent; it is ready to burst like new bottles.
And, again, strong drinks are not for the belly, but for the washing of your bodies.
Drink no longer water, but use a little wine for thy stomach’s sake and thine often infirmities.
And again, hot drinks are not for the body or belly.
I will speak, that I may be refreshed: I will open my lips and answer.
Hear; for I will speak of excellent things; and the opening of my lips shall be right things.
And it shall come to pass, that before they call, I will answer; and while they are yet speaking, I will hear.
Give ear, O ye heavens, and I will speak; and hear, O earth, the words of my mouth.
I will open my mouth in a parable: I will utter dark sayings of old:
Then call thou, and I will answer: or let me speak, and answer thou me.
Let me not, I pray you, accept any man’s person, neither let me give flattering titles unto man.
For I know not to give flattering titles; in so doing my maker would soon take me away.
Wherefore, let all men beware how they take my name in their lips—
And this I say, lest any man should beguile you with enticing words.
For I know not to give flattering titles; in so doing my maker would soon take me away.
Let me not, I pray you, accept any man’s person, neither let me give flattering titles unto man.
In my name they shall do many wonderful works;

'If the piece should be too long, it stands at the discretion
     of the manager to abbreviate the speculative parts of it, or
     here and there, without prejudice to the general impression,
     to omit them altogether. But in the _printing_, I use the
     freedom humbly to protest against the leaving out of
     anything. I had satisfactory reasons of my own for all that I
     allowed to…
